entries.mem0ai/mem0: The official Hermes Agent memory provider, offering a universal layer that retains user, session, and agent state. It utilizes a token-efficient ADD-only extraction algorithm and hybrid retrieval fusing semantic, keyword, and entity-linked signals.
entries.garrytan/gbrain: An opinionated brain for Hermes Agent featuring a self-wiring knowledge graph that extracts typed entity links without LLM calls. It combines vector and graph search with backlink-boosted ranking and exposes over 30 MCP tools.
entries.vectorize-io/hindsight: A learning-centric memory system that implements retain, recall, and reflect workflows for long-term agent improvement. It simplifies integration via a two-line LLM wrapper and ensures privacy through per-user metadata filtering.
entries.plastic-labs/honcho: A stateful memory library that maintains evolving context for users, agents, and groups over time. It supports Python and TypeScript SDKs and enables natural language querying to extract insights from interaction history.
entries.greyhaven-ai/autocontext: A recursive context harness designed for complex tasks through iterative improvement and structured traces. It leverages multi-role cooperation for strategy refinement and knowledge curation across various LLM providers.
entries.stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm: A lossless context management plugin that uses a hierarchical DAG structure for depth-aware conversation summarization. It ensures no message is lost by utilizing a SQLite-backed store for persistent raw history recovery.
entries.elkimek/honcho-self-hosted: A self-hosted backend that brings Honcho's memory capabilities to Hermes Agent for private, cross-session persistence. It includes automated Docker, PostgreSQL, and Redis setup scripts and supports local inference servers.
entries.yoloshii/ClawMem: A local-first context engine that operates entirely on-device without cloud dependencies or API keys. It employs hybrid retrieval using BM25, vector search, and knowledge graphs via MCP server integration.
entries.EfficientContext/ContextPilot: A context optimization tool that accelerates inference by deduplicating redundant blocks and reordering prefixes to boost cache hits. It provides native integration for Hermes Agent, OpenClaw, and vLLM.
entries.plur-ai/plur: A shared memory layer for multi-agent systems that stores data locally using an open YAML engram format. It offers zero-cost memory injection for Hermes and Claude Code using hybrid BM25 and embedding search.
entries.keepnotes-ai/keep: A reflective memory system that indexes codebases and media using automatic summarization and OCR. It creates navigable, bidirectional relationships between notes via edge tags and integrates through MCP and CLI interfaces.
entries.AxDSan/mnemosyne: A zero-dependency memory system optimized for sub-millisecond read, write, and search latency. It uses a local-first SQLite architecture with native vector and full-text search capabilities.
entries.amanning3390/flowstate-qmd: An anticipatory memory system that prefetches context by monitoring agent session logs. It utilizes a local-first stack with SQLite-vec and GGUF models, delivered as an MCP-native server.

entries.mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills: This repository provides 754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to major frameworks like MITRE ATT&CK and NIST CSF 2.0. It enables Hermes to perform specialized tasks in cloud security, forensics, and malware analysis using the agentskills.io standard.
entries.AMAP-ML/SkillClaw: SkillClaw introduces an 'Agentic Evolver' that automates skill deduplication and quality improvement through background evolution loops. It is designed to unify skill libraries across multiple agents and devices, offering native integration for Hermes and OpenClaw.
entries.conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing: This tool audits and rewrites content to eliminate 36 distinct AI writing patterns and structural tells. It uses a three-tier word replacement table and a two-pass audit to ensure Hermes produces more natural, human-like prose.
entries.wondelai/skills: A cross-platform library that provides modular skills for architecture, UX design, and product strategy via a dedicated CLI. It allows Hermes to quickly adopt specialized professional frameworks compatible with the agentskills.io specification.
entries.DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills: This project implements type-safe support for the Agent Skills open format specifically for Pydantic AI. It optimizes Hermes' performance by using progressive disclosure of metadata to significantly reduce token consumption.
entries.Agents365-ai/drawio-skill: This skill enables Hermes to generate complex draw.io diagrams from natural language descriptions using specialized presets for machine learning and system architecture. It features automated self-checking loops and supports advanced elements like animated connectors.
entries.Cranot/super-hermes: Super-Hermes teaches the agent to write its own analytical prompts and custom thinking instructions for complex problem-solving. It helps Hermes identify structural conservation laws in code and generate reports on its own analysis limitations.
entries.smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills: This library equips Hermes with standardized skills for interacting with Chainlink's oracle network, including CCIP, VRF, and Data Feeds. It follows the agentskills.io spec to facilitate automated smart contract operations and runtime management.
entries.tlehman/litprog-skill: This skill brings literate programming to Hermes, allowing it to weave runnable code and prose together into .lit.md files. It includes a 'tangler' to extract source files and automatically syncs edits between documentation and code.
entries.esaradev/icarus-plugin: Icarus provides Hermes with cross-instance shared memory and a pipeline for training its own replacement models. It uses markdown-based directories to store decision-quality data, enabling the agent to remember work across different sessions.
entries.chigwell/skilldock.io: Skilldock is a registry and SDK for managing the lifecycle of reusable AI skills with recursive dependency resolution. It uniquely integrates commerce rails, allowing for skill transactions using USD or the TON blockchain.
entries.black-forest-labs/skills: These official skills provide Hermes with precise integration patterns and prompting guidelines for the FLUX image generation model family. They support structured JSON prompting and exact hex color specifications for high-fidelity visual outputs.
entries.Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory: This meta-skill plugin monitors active workflows to automatically detect and propose new, reusable skills. It generates the necessary Python scaffolds and documentation, allowing Hermes to expand its own capabilities dynamically.
entries.ReinaMacCredy/maestro: Maestro is a harness for long-running missions that provides Hermes with structured local memory and a TUI dashboard for real-time tracking. It facilitates multi-agent collaboration through formal handoffs and markdown-based mission briefs.
entries.tiann/execplan-skill: This skill enables Hermes to handle complex, multi-step tasks by implementing execution checkpoints and autonomous recovery procedures. It ensures reliability for long-running processes through real-time decision tracking and built-in rollbacks.
entries.cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill: This progressive MCP client uses a three-layer lazy loading architecture to minimize token usage during tool discovery. It maintains persistent server connections via a daemon, allowing Hermes to hot-reload configurations without interruption.
entries.armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge: Skill-Forge converts existing code repositories and developer documentation into agentskills.io-compliant skills. It provides a verifiable audit trail with line-level provenance to ensure Hermes uses accurate, version-pinned information.
entries.amanning3390/hermeshub: HermesHub acts as a community marketplace for browsing and installing skills with built-in security scanning and threat detection. It features an agent-to-agent feedback system to establish trust scores for different community-contributed skills.
entries.PederHP/skillsdotnet: This project provides a C# and .NET implementation of the agentskills.io standard for integration with Microsoft.Extensions.AI. It allows .NET developers to load skills into Hermes using the progressive disclosure pattern via SKILL.md files.
entries.adnw-vinc/hermes-nextcloud: This skill connects Hermes to Nextcloud environments to manage files, calendars, and contacts via WebDAV and CalDAV protocols. It includes a guided setup script to ensure secure communication using Nextcloud App Passwords.

entries.numtide/llm-agents.nix: This project provides a comprehensive collection of Nix packages for AI coding agents, including Hermes, ensuring they are always up-to-date through automated daily builds. It allows developers to execute agents instantly via Nix flakes, offering a unified and reproducible environment for CLI-based AI tools.
entries.TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw: Hermesclaw focuses on high-security deployments by implementing hardware-level sandboxing through the NVIDIA OpenShell kernel. It offers multi-layer protection that strictly enforces network and filesystem policies, making it the primary choice for running Hermes in sensitive or untrusted environments.
entries.0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent: This repository provides a dedicated NixOS module for the declarative management of Hermes configurations, documents, and systemd services. It excels at secure secret handling via sops-nix integration, catering to users who require a reproducible, system-level deployment on Nix-based infrastructure.
entries.rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent: Designed for Windows users, this zero-install solution provides a GUI-driven desktop agent equipped with 100 built-in tools and local model support. It eliminates the need for Docker or complex installations, featuring a dynamic tool maker and workflow engine for local automation pipelines.
entries.xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker: This project offers a streamlined Docker image that bundles the official Hermes installation with mini-swe-agent for a ready-to-use sandbox. It supports persistent state through mounted directories and allows users to target specific Hermes versions using simple build arguments.
entries.jpalmae/hermeshq: HermesHQ serves as a centralized Docker-first control plane for orchestrating multiple agent instances and workspaces from a single web interface. It introduces advanced features like inter-agent delegation, automated task scheduling, and native integrations for Telegram and WhatsApp.
entries.JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server: Targeting production environments on Ubuntu and Debian, this project configures the Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service. It is optimized for headless operation and autonomous execution, enabling the agent to perform recurring AI cron jobs without manual intervention.
entries.ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes: This deployment stack integrates Hermes with a ttyd web terminal, allowing users to interact with the agent's CLI directly through a browser. It is designed for easy management via Portainer and utilizes GitHub Actions for automated image builds and deployment.

entries.builderz-labs/mission-control: This self-hosted orchestration platform manages multi-agent workflows across frameworks like CrewAI and AutoGen with zero external dependencies. It is essential for Hermes users requiring local task dispatching, cost monitoring, and human-in-the-loop sign-offs via the Aegis review system.
entries.swarmclawai/swarmclaw: Swarmclaw enables the creation of autonomous agent swarms with native support for Hermes and over 20 other LLM providers. It distinguishes itself through durable agent memory and a visual organization chart that tracks how agents delegate tasks and learn new skills from conversations.
entries.howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness: This specialized framework uses an automated outer-loop search process to optimize the harness code used for evaluating Hermes Agent. It orchestrates complex benchmark evaluations, such as TBLite, by mutating and comparing candidate code to find the most effective performance frontiers.
entries.runtimenoteslabs/gladiator: Gladiator creates a competitive arena where two autonomous AI companies, powered by Hermes Agent, compete for GitHub stars. The system orchestrates nine distinct agents and provides a real-time dashboard to visualize task execution, code generation, and live scoreboard metrics.
entries.1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent: This project coordinates a pipeline of 17 specialized agents focused on research, infrastructure, and security. It ensures high-standard outputs by enforcing mandatory quality gates where reviewer and tester agents must approve work before it moves through the master orchestrator.
entries.supermodeltools/bigiron: Bigiron automates an eight-phase software development lifecycle by combining Hermes Agent with Supermodel MCP code graphs. It maintains architectural integrity by using graph-diff phase gates to validate structural changes at every step from planning to health checks.
entries.Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill: This framework implements ACP-style delegation to route tasks between Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code. It focuses on operational safety by isolating delegation workflows to prevent context pollution and enforcing strict timeouts and output limits.

entries.junhoyeo/tokscale: This high-performance Rust TUI tracks token consumption and costs across multiple AI coding assistants, including Hermes Agent and Claude Code. It serves as a centralized dashboard for developers to monitor their usage metrics and spend across various model providers in real-time.
entries.joeynyc/hermes-skins: This utility allows users to personalize the Hermes terminal interface through YAML-based configuration files for ASCII banners, spinners, and color schemes. It enables the creation and sharing of community-driven visual themes that inherit from default settings for easy customization.
entries.AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC: A native port of the Hermes Agent brain to the Claude Code Channel, this tool integrates 46 core skills directly into the environment without requiring external processes or OAuth. It specifically adds MLOps capabilities for vLLM, GGUF inference, and reinforcement learning training directly within the developer's workflow.
entries.unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser: Designed for autonomous agent control via MCP, this AI-native browser provides a visible interface for human supervision and session management. It features action rollback capabilities and automatically infers structured schemas for web elements like forms and products to improve agent navigation.
entries.Abruptive/Ankh.md: This framework enables the creation of scoped Hermes Agents that are restricted to specific project folders with local configurations and memories. It allows developers to seamlessly switch between global settings and project-specific instructions, tools, and skills.
entries.roli-lpci/lintlang: A deterministic static linter that validates AI agent configurations and system prompts without incurring LLM costs. It uses regex and heuristics to detect seven structural failure modes, ensuring schema-intent alignment for Hermes-compatible YAML and JSON files in CI/CD pipelines.
entries.0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes: This migration utility automates the transition from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent by mapping personas, memories, and complex model configurations to the Hermes YAML format. It securely handles the transfer of credentials and workspace files from local environment variables to the new stack.
entries.42-evey/evey-setup: This deployment script uses Docker to quickly stand up a full Hermes Agent stack integrated with LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant. It comes pre-configured with 29 community plugins and is optimized for zero-cost local GPU inference and automated model routing.

entries.nesquena/hermes-webui: This lightweight Python and vanilla JavaScript interface brings full feature parity with the Hermes CLI to mobile and web browsers. It features a streamlined three-panel layout that integrates workspace file browsing with inline previews for a seamless remote experience.
entries.EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui: A comprehensive management dashboard that centralizes configuration for messaging channels like Telegram, Discord, and Slack. It provides deep operational oversight through real-time token analytics, cost tracking, and an integrated web terminal for backend administration.
entries.outsourc-e/hermes-workspace: Designed for zero-fork compatibility with vanilla installations, this workspace provides a unified interface for chat, terminal access, and memory browsing. It is particularly notable for its Swarm Mode, which enables role-based dispatching of multiple persistent agents.
entries.xaspx/hermes-control-interface: This self-hosted gateway acts as a command center for starting, stopping, and configuring individual agent profiles. It includes a robust xterm.js terminal with full PTY support alongside detailed analytics for monitoring model distribution and platform usage costs.
entries.dodo-reach/hermes-desktop: A native macOS application that establishes direct SSH connections to Hermes environments without requiring remote helper services. It offers a Mac-centric workflow for session management, including Kanban boards and remote file editing for named agent profiles.
entries.clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp: This mobile-first React dashboard operates as a Telegram Mini App, offering a terminal-style chat interface with slash command support. It allows users to spawn up to five concurrent background agents while monitoring system resources through live CPU and memory gauges.
entries.aivrar/portable-hermes-agent: A zero-install Windows GUI that enables local agent execution without Docker or administrative rights. It features a library of over 100 tools for TTS and image generation, plus a dynamic tool maker for building custom API wrappers at runtime.
entries.fathah/hermes-desktop: This desktop companion simplifies the onboarding process with a guided installer for local dependencies and environment setup. The interface provides a centralized hub for managing 16 messaging gateways and 14 toolsets, complete with full-text search for session history.
entries.sanchomuzax/hermes-webui: Focused on observability, this dashboard provides a real-time activity feed for monitoring active sessions and scheduled cron jobs. It utilizes FTS5 search for deep inspection of conversation histories and offers read-only configuration viewing to track costs.
entries.Euraika-Labs/pan-ui: A chat-centric workspace that emphasizes extensibility through an integrated marketplace for skills and MCP servers. It provides detailed tool execution timelines and profile-scoped management for global memory and agent runtime controls.
entries.pyrate-llama/hermes-ui: This glassmorphic React application offers a modern aesthetic for self-hosted AI assistance, powered by a lightweight Python proxy. It combines real-time log streaming and task tracking with an integrated file browser and MCP tool management interface.
